verses.fhb_text: Now, priests, this command is for you.
verses.decision_notes: Opening address to the priests; the Father speaks. No divine name in the verse, so no Person-resolution needed.

verses.fhb_text: If you will not listen, and if you will not take it to heart to give honor to my name, says the Father of heaven's armies, then I will send the curse upon you, and I will curse your blessings. Indeed, I have already cursed them, because you do not take it to heart.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The Father warns the priests in covenant-discipline language (Father-language applies to discipline per FHB-6.2.1-1).

Renderings:
• "the Father of heaven's armies" — YHWH-Tzva'ot with no Adonai present, per FHB-4.4.1-2
• "my name" — Father self-reference, first person; no padded Father form per FHB-1.2-31

verses.fhb_text: I am about to rebuke your offspring, and I will spread manure upon your faces — the manure of your feasts — and you will be swept away with it.
verses.decision_notes: Father speaking in first person; vivid judgment imagery preserved (FHB-7.8-1). "Offspring" (zera) kept as the seed the priests teach and lead.

verses.fhb_text: Then you will know that I sent this command to you, that my covenant with Levi might stand, says the Father of heaven's armies.
verses.decision_notes: "the Father of heaven's armies" for YHWH-Tzva'ot (FHB-4.4.1-2). Father self-references "I" / "my covenant" (FHB-1.2-31).

verses.fhb_text: My covenant with him was life and peace, and I gave them to him to be held with reverence; and he revered me and stood in awe of my name.
verses.decision_notes: Father recalls the Levitical covenant in first person. "Revered me... before my name" — fear/awe rendered as relational reverence toward the Father.

verses.fhb_text: True instruction was in his mouth, and no wrong was found on his lips. He walked with me in peace and uprightness, and he turned many away from sin.
verses.decision_notes: Father speaking of faithful Levi. "Walked with me" — covenant-walk intimacy in the Father's own voice.

verses.fhb_text: For the lips of a priest should guard knowledge, and people should seek instruction from his mouth, for he is the messenger of the Father of heaven's armies.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The priest is the Father's messenger to the people.

Renderings:
• "messenger of the Father of heaven's armies" — mal'akh of YHWH-Tzva'ot; "messenger" lowercase (a human priest, not a theophany figure) per FHB-1.2-8 distinction; YHWH-Tzva'ot rendered per FHB-4.4.1-2

verses.fhb_text: But you have turned aside from the way; you have made many stumble by your instruction; you have corrupted the covenant of Levi, says the Father of heaven's armies.
verses.decision_notes: Father indicts the priests; "the Father of heaven's armies" for YHWH-Tzva'ot (FHB-4.4.1-2).

verses.fhb_text: So I, in turn, have made you despised and humbled before all the people, because you have not kept my ways but have shown partiality in your instruction.
verses.decision_notes: Father speaking judgment on the priests in first person (FHB-1.2-31); covenant-discipline Father-language (FHB-6.2.1-1).

verses.fhb_text: Do we not all have one Father? Did not one Father create us? Why then are we faithless to one another, profaning the covenant of our fathers?
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Malachi grounds his whole argument in the family-relationship language — one Father, one Maker.

Renderings:
• "one Father" — av echad preserved; the prophet's explicit Fatherhood claim
• "one God create us" — el echad bera'anu; the deliberate Father∥Creator parallel preserved per the FHB-1.2-27 Mal 2:10 worked example

Speaker is Malachi addressing the people.

verses.fhb_text: Judah has been faithless, and an abomination has been committed in Israel and in Jerusalem. For Judah has profaned the holy place of our Father, which he loves, and has married the daughter of a foreign god.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Covenant unfaithfulness profanes the Father's sanctuary.

Renderings:
• "the holy place of our Father" — YHWH (narrator/prophet voice) resolves to our Father per FHB-1.2-31
• "daughter of a foreign god" — bat el nechar preserved; a pagan deity in the idolatry frame (FHB-2.9-1), not the Father

verses.fhb_text: May our Father cut off from the tents of Jacob the man who does this — whoever he may be — even though he brings an offering to the Father of heaven's armies.
verses.decision_notes:
Renderings:
• "our Father" — YHWH in the prophet's imprecation (FHB-1.2-31)
• "the Father of heaven's armies" — YHWH-Tzva'ot (FHB-4.4.1-2)

Em dash pair for the difficult idiom 'er v'oneh' (whoever he may be); single pair per FHB-9.5-1.
verses.footnote: 'Tents of Jacob' means the whole community of Israel.

verses.fhb_text: And this is a second thing you do: you cover the altar of our Father with tears, with weeping and groaning, because he no longer pays attention to the offering or accepts it gladly from your hand.
verses.decision_notes: "the altar of our Father" — YHWH resolves to our Father in the prophet's voice (FHB-1.2-31).

verses.fhb_text: But you ask, "Why?" Because our Father was a witness between you and the wife of your youth, to whom you have been faithless, though she is your companion and your wife by covenant.
verses.decision_notes: "our Father was a witness" — YHWH resolves to our Father (FHB-1.2-31). Marriage as covenant the Father witnesses.

verses.fhb_text: Didn't the Father make you one with your wife? In body and spirit you are his. And what does he want? God-fearing children from your union. So guard yourself in your spirit, and let none be faithless to the wife of his youth.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: A famously difficult verse; FHB preserves its ambiguity rather than forcing a single reading.

Renderings:
• "a remnant of spirit belonging to him" — she'ar ruach; rendered the least-committal faithful way per FHB-1.2-33 (mainstream versions also diverge here)
• "Godly offspring" — zera elohim; Elohim as genitive of quality ("godly"), the divine act of seeking holy seed (FHB-1.2-30)

Hebrew: zera elohim (offspring of God) → "godly offspring"

Kevin 2026-06-06: relational rendering adopted (the Father made you one with your wife; God-fearing children). Hebrew ha'echad referent = the Father.

verses.fhb_text: "For I hate divorce," says our Father, the Father of Israel, "and the one who stains his garment with violence," says the Father of heaven's armies. So guard yourselves in your spirit, and do not be faithless.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The Father's heart against the breaking of the marriage covenant.

Renderings:
• "I hate divorce" — rendered as the Father's first-person declaration per FHB-1.2-25 (Mal 2:16 worked example)
• "our Father, the God of Israel" — YHWH resolves to our Father; 'Elohei Yisrael' kept as the covenant title (FHB-1.2-31)
• "the Father of heaven's armies" — YHWH-Tzva'ot (FHB-4.4.1-2)

verses.fhb_text: You have wearied our Father with your words. But you ask, "How have we wearied him?" By saying, "Everyone who does evil is good in his sight, and in them he delights" — or by asking, "Where is the Father of justice?"
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The people's cynical words weary the Father, who longs to be honored.

Renderings:
• "wearied our Father" — prophet's narration; YHWH → our Father (FHB-1.2-31)
• "good in the sight of our Father" — YHWH on the complacent covenant-insiders' lips still resolves to our Father, sharpening the irony (FHB-4.1-1)
• "the God of justice" — Elohei hamishpat; generic deity-term in the skeptics' challenge preserved as 'God' (FHB-4.1-1 lexical rule)

chapter_notes: Malachi 2 divides between the Father's address to the priests (vv.1-9, first-person divine speech) and Malachi's address to the people (vv.10-17). YHWH-Tzva'ot is rendered 'the Father of heaven's armies' throughout (FHB-4.4.1-2, no Adonai present). Bare YHWH resolves to 'our Father' in the prophet's narration and to first-person self-reference in the Father's speech (FHB-1.2-31). v.10 preserves the explicit 'one Father / one God' parallel per the FHB-1.2-27 worked example. v.16 renders the Father's first-person 'I hate divorce' per FHB-1.2-25. The 'God of Israel' (v.16) and 'God of justice' (v.17, on skeptics' lips) are preserved as covenant title and generic challenge respectively.
flags_summary: Layer 3 fidelity self-check: 1 verse(s) checked — 1 confirmed, 0 revised (applied), 0 suggested, 0 unresolved

Malachi 2:15 — translation rests on a mainstream-consensus reading of a genuinely contested verse (subject of 'make one', 'remnant of spirit', identity of 'the One'); governed by FHB-1.2-33 but surfaced for awareness given the textual difficulty.
